factors.summary: Demand is accelerating, particularly in pool and water treatment businesses.

evidence.text: • increased sales volume in our pool business due to consumers increasing their pool use while continuing to invest in their homes and backyards; • increased sales volume in our water treatment business as residential demand remained strong and commercial demand improved due to recovery in the restaurant and hospitality industries in 2021; • increases in selling prices to mitigate impacts of inflation; • increased sales due to the Pleatco and KBI acquisitions that occurred in 2021; and • favorable foreign currency effects in 2021 compared to the prior year.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The evidence shows strong growth across multiple business segments with favorable factors like increased sales and favorable currency effects.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices to mitigate inflationary costs.

evidence.text: • increases in selling prices to mitigate impacts of inflation; • increases in selling prices to mitigate a rise in inflationary costs; • increases in selling prices to mitigate the impacts of inflation.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The company has successfully implemented price increases to counter inflation, with multiple evidence points supporting this.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, excluding acquisitions.

evidence.text: • volume increase in our Consumer Solutions segment mainly driven by continued robust demand in our pool and water treatment businesses; • volume increase in our Industrial & Flow Technologies segment primarily driven by strong demand in the residential and irrigation flow businesses as well as increased demand and recovery in our food and beverage and commercial flow businesses; • increases in selling prices to mitigate a rise in inflationary costs; and • favorable foreign currency effects in 2021 compared to the prior year.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, driven by volume increases and pricing actions, indicating a positive trend.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing across both Consumer Solutions and Industrial & Flow Technologies segments.

evidence.text: • volume increase in our Consumer Solutions segment mainly driven by continued robust demand in our pool and water treatment businesses; • volume increase in our Industrial & Flow Technologies segment primarily driven by strong demand in the residential and irrigation flow businesses as well as increased demand and recovery in our food and beverage and commercial flow businesses.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing across both segments, reflecting strong demand and positive market momentum.

factors.summary: End markets are growing fastest in pool, water treatment, and food and beverage businesses.

evidence.text: • increased sales volume in our pool business due to consumers increasing their pool use while continuing to invest in their homes and backyards; • increased sales volume in our water treatment business as residential demand remained strong and commercial demand improved due to recovery in the restaurant and hospitality industries in 2021; • increased sales volume in our food and beverage business in 2021 due to demand in sustainable gas solutions and beer membrane filtration systems along with recovery in short cycle orders; • increased sales volume in our commercial flow business due to demand in our water supply and water disposal product lines.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: End markets are growing rapidly in key sectors, indicating strong demand and positive business momentum.

factors.summary: Raw material and component costs are rising due to tight supply and inflation.

evidence.text: • inflationary cost increases due to tight supply of raw materials such as metals, resins and electronics (including drives and motors); • higher logistics and labor costs due to availability constraints and high demand; • increased productivity in both the Consumer Solutions and Industrial & Flow Technologies segments.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising raw material and component costs due to tight supply and inflation are creating headwinds for margins.

factors.summary: Labor costs are creating margin pressure due to wage increases and workforce shortages.

evidence.text: • higher logistics and labor costs due to increased demand as well as factory labor wage increases; • increased logistics and labor costs due to availability constraints and high demand; • labor costs due to workforce shortages.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are increasing due to wage hikes and shortages, putting pressure on margins.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding due to volume growth and pricing actions.

evidence.text: • gross profit as a percentage of net sales was unchanged when comparing 2021 and 2020, primarily as a result of: • volume expansion in both our Consumer Solutions and Industrial & Flow Technologies segments; • increases in selling prices to mitigate impacts of inflation; • complexity reduction and exiting older, less profitable product lines in our residential and commercial flow businesses in our Industrial & Flow Technologies segment; and • increased productivity in both the Consumer Solutions and Industrial & Flow Technologies segments.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are expanding due to volume growth, pricing actions, and productivity improvements.

factors.summary: Cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings.

evidence.text: • transformation costs that primarily represented professional services and project management related charges; • incurred transformation costs that include professional services, project management and related design and execution charges, as well as costs related to both labor and non-labor restructuring and IT investments.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings, indicating positive progress.

factors.summary: Operational efficiency is improving due to cost actions and productivity gains.

evidence.text: • increased productivity in both the Consumer Solutions and Industrial & Flow Technologies segments; • increased productivity due to cost actions driving manufacturing efficiencies.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Operational efficiency improvements due to cost actions and productivity gains suggest positive developments.

factors.summary: Management is confident, as evidenced by buyback commitments and capital expenditures.

evidence.text: • Return cash to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases; and • Supplement our business with strategically-aligned mergers and acquisitions.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management confidence is evident through buyback commitments and capital expenditures, indicating a positive outlook.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include supply chain challenges and inflationary pressures.

evidence.text: • supply chain challenges, including availability constraints and high demand; • inflationary increases of raw materials such as metals, resins and electronics (including drives and motors), as well as increases in logistics and labor costs.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include significant supply chain challenges and inflationary pressures, indicating clear negative factors.

factors.summary: Long-term strategic direction is clear with the Transformation Program.

evidence.text: • During 2021, we launched and committed resources to a program designed to accelerate growth and drive margin expansion through transformation across our businesses to elevate our capabilities, reduce complexity and streamline our processes (the “Transformation Program”).

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: A clear long-term strategic direction with the Transformation Program shows very positive developments.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include raw material costs, foreign exchange rates, and supply chain disruptions.

evidence.text: • inflationary increases of raw materials such as metals, resins and electronics (including drives and motors), as well as increases in logistics and labor costs; • favorable foreign currency effects in 2021 compared to the prior year; • supply chain challenges, including increased lead times for raw materials due to availability constraints and high demand.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Key sensitivities include manageable risks like raw material costs and foreign exchange rates, which are neutral in sentiment.

factors.summary: Catalysts include the Transformation Program and successful acquisitions.

evidence.text: • During 2021, we launched and committed resources to a program designed to accelerate growth and drive margin expansion through transformation across our businesses to elevate our capabilities, reduce complexity and streamline our processes (the “Transformation Program”); • We executed certain business restructuring initiatives unrelated to the COVID-19 pandemic aimed at reducing our fixed cost structure and realigning our business.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Catalysts such as the Transformation Program and successful acquisitions indicate positive opportunities.

factors.summary: Supply chain health is still disrupted with ongoing challenges.

evidence.text: • supply chain challenges, including availability constraints and high demand; • increased lead times for raw materials due to availability constraints and high demand; • supply chain challenges, including availability of materials, increased lead times, as well as inflation of raw materials, logistics and labor costs due to availability constraints and high demand.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Ongoing supply chain disruptions and challenges indicate very negative sentiment.

factors.summary: Manufacturing capacity utilization is stable.

evidence.text: • Our businesses generally have been and continue to be considered essential under applicable government-mandated orders which has allowed us substantially to maintain business continuity at substantially all of our manufacturing facilities throughout the COVID-19 pandemic.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The summary indicates stability in manufacturing capacity utilization, supported by maintaining business continuity during the pandemic.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are normalizing.

evidence.text: • We have elevated our engagement with our suppliers and used secondary suppliers and new methods of procurement where available to mitigate the supply chain pressures; • We expect supply chain challenges to continue in 2022, and which may continue thereafter and could negatively impact our results of operations.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Inventory levels are normalizing, but ongoing supply chain challenges may negatively impact operations, leading to a mixed sentiment.

factors.summary: Market share is improving due to strong demand and strategic initiatives.

evidence.text: • increased sales volume in our pool business due to consumers increasing their pool use while continuing to invest in their homes and backyards; • increased sales volume in our water treatment business as residential demand remained strong and commercial demand improved due to recovery in the restaurant and hospitality industries in 2021.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Market share is improving due to strong demand and strategic initiatives, supported by increased sales in key businesses.

factors.summary: Competitive advantages include technology and scale.

evidence.text: • We are a pure play water industrial manufacturing company comprised of two reporting segments: Consumer Solutions and Industrial & Flow Technologies; • We have a strong focus on operational excellence, reducing complexity, and streamlining processes.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Competitive advantages include technology and scale, supported by a focus on operational excellence and streamlining processes.

factors.summary: R&D investment is part of the strategy to drive growth.

evidence.text: • focused growth initiatives that accelerate our investments in digital, technology and services expansion; • delivering decision making speed through organizational clarity and improved processes; • developing a future state digital enterprise; and • modernizing general and administrative capabilities.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: R&D investment is part of a strategy to drive growth, supported by accelerating investments in digital and technology.

factors.summary: Digital technologies and automation are being adopted to drive efficiency.

evidence.text: • delivering decision making speed through organizational clarity and improved processes; • developing a future state digital enterprise; and • modernizing general and administrative capabilities.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Digital technologies and automation are being adopted to drive efficiency, supported by developing a future state digital enterprise.

factors.summary: New products are performing well, contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: • increased sales due to the Pleatco and KBI acquisitions that occurred in 2021; • We have identified specific product and geographic market opportunities that we find attractive and continue to pursue, both within and outside the U.S.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: New products are performing well, contributing to revenue growth, supported by increased sales and identified opportunities.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are favorable, supporting business growth.

evidence.text: • overall global economic and business conditions impacting our business, including the strength of housing and related markets; • the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on the global economy, our workforce, customers and suppliers, and customer demand; • the impact of seasonality of sales and weather conditions.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Economic conditions are favorable, supporting business growth, despite some challenges from the pandemic and seasonality.

factors.summary: Interest rate changes are benefiting the company through lower borrowing costs.

evidence.text: • The 47.7 percent decrease in net interest expense in 2021 from 2020 was the result of: • strong cash flows in 2021 used to reduce overall debt levels during the year compared to 2020; and • lower interest rates on outstanding variable rate debt.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Lower borrowing costs due to reduced debt and lower interest rates have significantly decreased net interest expense.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are a risk due to currency fluctuations.

evidence.text: • favorable foreign currency effects in 2021 compared to the prior year; • unfavorable foreign currency effects compared to the prior year.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed foreign currency effects indicate no clear directional signal.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are increasing to support growth.

evidence.text: • Return cash to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases; and • Supplement our business with strategically-aligned mergers and acquisitions.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Evidence does not clearly indicate a trend in capital expenditures.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is improving due to reduced debt levels.

evidence.text: • The 47.7 percent decrease in net interest expense in 2021 from 2020 was the result of: • strong cash flows in 2021 used to reduce overall debt levels during the year compared to 2020; and • lower interest rates on outstanding variable rate debt.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Reduced debt levels and lower interest rates have improved debt leverage.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are being prioritized through dividends and buybacks.

evidence.text: • Return cash to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases; and • Supplement our business with strategically-aligned mergers and acquisitions.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Active return of cash through dividends and buybacks prioritizes shareholder value.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving.

evidence.text: • Return cash to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases; and • Supplement our business with strategically-aligned mergers and acquisitions.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong cash flows support shareholder returns and indicate improving free cash flow generation.

factors.summary: Mergers and acquisitions are creating value.

evidence.text: • We completed the acquisition of Pleatco Holdings, LLC and related entities (“Pleatco”) for $254.6 million in cash, net of cash acquired. Pleatco manufactures water filtration and clean air technologies for pool, spa and industrial air customers; • We completed the acquisition of Ken’s Beverage, Inc. (“KBI”) for $83.1 million in cash, net of cash acquired. KBI provides beverage equipment and services to commercial customers.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strategic mergers and acquisitions indicate value creation through growth initiatives.

factors.summary: Headcount restructuring is part of the strategy to reduce costs.

evidence.text: • We executed certain business restructuring initiatives unrelated to the COVID-19 pandemic aimed at reducing our fixed cost structure and realigning our business. We expect these actions to continue into 2022 and to drive margin growth.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring initiatives aimed at reducing costs and driving margin growth.

factors.summary: Labor relations are stable with enhanced safety protocols.

evidence.text: • In light of the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic, we maintain our commitment to protect the health and safety of our employees by continuing our enhanced safety protocols for those on-site at our manufacturing facilities, for those who provide manufacturing-support activities, and for those working in office environments.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Enhanced safety protocols indicate positive steps towards improving labor relations.
